title: Kazuma's Marketing Empire (Don't Let Them Repo The Urus)
statement: After blowing three Topstep funded accounts, Kazuma, Aqua, Megumin, and Darkness pooled their remaining payout to lease a Lamborghini Urus together for content. The plan: funnel clout across their marketing channels and sell enough subscriptions of their paid course "The Isekai Trading Matrix ($125 monthly)" on Whop before the repo man shows up.

Their marketing network has n platforms and m bidirectional collab deals. Each deal between platforms u and v costs c coins to activate, cross-promotions aren't free. They start at platform 1 (their broke Discord server) and need to reach platform n (Course Sales). Platforms are interconnected in a web of clout with possible cycles; a collab deal can be revisited, but that would just waste money.
Kazuma has exactly B coins left. Find the minimum marketing cost to reach Course Sales. If it is impossible to reach or the minimum cost exceeds B, print -1. Aqua will cry either way.
input_format: The first line contains a single integer t (1 ≤ t ≤ 10<sup>4</sup>), the number of test cases.

Each test case begins with three integers n, m, and B. The next m lines each contain three integers u, v, c (1 ≤ u, v ≤ n, u ≠ v, 1 ≤ c ≤ 10<sup>9</sup>) describing a bidirectional edge between platforms u and v with cost c.
output_format: For each test case, print the minimum cost to travel from platform 1 to platform n, or -1 if it is impossible or exceeds B.
constraints: Nodes are numbered 1 to n (1-indexed). Edges are bidirectional.
1 ≤ u, v ≤ n for each edge, u ≠ v. No self-loops.
Graph may contain cycles. All edge weights are positive.
Sum of n and m across all test cases ≤ 2×10<sup>5</sup>
notes: Test 1: path 1 to 2 to 4 to 5 costs 5+3+7 = 15. Urus safe. Test 2: cheapest path costs 13, which exceeds B = 5. Repo man wins.
